summary: Independent read-only QA review accepts M4-ROUTE-TRANSPORT-CAPACITY-001 after systems_architect ACCEPT. The actual branch/worktree diff stays within allowed protocol, sim-core, tests, task, and project-message paths; the implementation adds a narrow read-only sim.preview-m4-route-transport-capacity query, reuses M2 route/season preview behavior, reports route segments, bottleneck capacity, travel window, carried supply limit, overload and season reason codes, and does not introduce UI, march execution, battle, siege, full economy, save-format, naval expansion, new dependencies, or production path drift. Required validation commands were rerun successfully. The new route-capacity test file is still untracked and must be staged by lead integration; the systems ACCEPT handoff is tracked in this branch but is new relative to origin/main and should remain included.

decisions: ACCEPT. The required acceptance criteria are satisfied by the actual diff and rerun commands.

The route/capacity behavior is deterministic: route choice delegates to the accepted M2 preview implementation with stable directed-edge and route-id/direction tie-breakers, and the new M4 tests verify stable output across shuffled route and reservation ordering.

Capacity and overload behavior is covered: tests assert route segments, bottleneck capacity, carried supply limit, capacity-exceeded status, overload reason code, unreachable route handling, and read-only no-side-effect behavior.
Monsoon/season behavior is covered by deterministic repeated-query assertions and explicit reason-code ordering for monsoon and road delay risks.
The new query uses M4 grain reservations for carried supply and M4 mobilized force commitments for plannedTroops; future march/supply execution remains outside this task.
Water transport remains only the existing M2 routeKind/seasonal capacity modifier. No fleets, naval battles, amphibious operations, or independent water-route state were introduced.

No forbidden scope or path drift was found: no UI, desktop, save-format, client/map/ui/platform, content, march execution, battle, siege, full economy, server, Rust/WASM, telemetry, or new dependency changes.
risks: Integration risk: tests/sim-core-m4-route-transport-capacity.test.ts is untracked in the current worktree. If it is not staged, packages/sim-core/package.json will reference a missing test file in the integrated commit.
Process artifact risk: the systems ACCEPT handoff is tracked in this branch but new relative to origin/main; lead integration should keep it and this QA ACCEPT handoff with the task record.

Forecast semantics are intentionally planning-only. Aggregate carriedSupplyLimit sums per-source limits and aggregate bottleneckCapacity reports the minimum per-source bottleneck; future simultaneous convoy or shared-edge reservation behavior needs a separate task/ADR.
Polity-target route planning remains out of scope; the query intentionally rejects non-district campaign targets.
requested_action: Route this QA ACCEPT to lead_orchestrator. Lead can proceed with integration/acceptance after staging the modified protocol/sim-core/test files, the untracked tests/sim-core-m4-route-transport-capacity.test.ts, and this QA handoff, while preserving the systems ACCEPT handoff already tracked in the branch. Do not mark CLOSED until lead integration gates and staging checks include the untracked test.
